programme.desc: The birth of their daughter (Courteney Cox) and son's first baby throws hostile ex-friends (Suzanne Pleshette, Debbie Reynolds) together.

programme.title: Baby Sellers
programme.desc: Inspired by true events, this film follows the operations of a major U.S. adoption agency, exposing the dark crime enterprise of infant trafficking.

programme.title: And Baby Will Fall
programme.desc: Ivy and David have a yard sale to clear their home before their baby arrives. Among the bargain hunters is Melinda, who is pregnant and now missing.

programme.title: A Daughter's Conviction
programme.desc: The only person who believes a woman is innocent of murder is the daughter (Brooke Nevin), and she turns amateur sleuth to clear her mother's name.

programme.title: When Mom Becomes a Murderer
programme.desc: Following the murder of her best friend, an introverted teen discovers her mom has a secret past that involves a similar crime. Desperate to uncover the truth, she soon finds herself in over her head as she wonders if her mother is a killer.

programme.title: Dangerous Medicine
programme.desc: A physical therapist becomes obsessed with a new patient after helping him regain the use of his legs.

programme.title: Am I a Serial Killer?

programme.title: Crowdsource Murder
programme.desc: A friend encourages Monica to start a crowdsource fund to pay for her daughter's hospital bills. However, when an attractive and seemingly generous man donates more and more money, she begins to question his motives.

programme.desc: After her husband Eduardo is kidnapped near their ranch in Mexico, Jayne has to raise enough money to ransom him back.

programme.title: You Can't Take My Daughter

Discovering that she is pregnant from the rape, Amy makes the difficult decision to keep the baby. After giving birth, she decides to start over in Atlanta. Six years later, Demetri finds her and, to her horror, sues her for custody of her daughter. Using her own legal and investigative skills, Amy fights back.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Criminologists, forensic pathologists, federal agents, and other experts delve into the lives of the most terrifying and notorious killers in history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a young boy and his mother were kidnapped and murdered by a vicious drug cartel, FBI agents in the field worked tirelessly to pierce the secretive drug underworld and unearth those involved. Their surveillance and investigative work uncovered a violent trail of murder and greed. FBI agents and forensic examiners pieced together the evidence needed to draw out and then crush the cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Beginning in California in 1995, a serial killer targeted vulnerable women. At first charming and generous, he quickly became violent. He was brutal with his kills and quick in his getaways. Traveling across the country, he struck in Mississippi, Louisiana and Florida. The FBI and local police needed to stop this cross-country killer before more women fell victim to his insatiable rage.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: At first it seemed like a tragic hunting accident when 18-year-old David Wilkey was shot to death in rural Virginia in 1983. A tenacious insurance investigator suspected foul play, but he had no direct evidence. Two years later a man contacted North Carolina FBI agents with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire: he claimed he had been hired to murder Wilkey's killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, the decapitation of a young African-American male led the Miami-Dade police department and the FBI along a trail of murder. Investigators quickly discovered that the victim was a dissident member of a religious sect headed by a man calling himself Yahweh ben Yahweh, or God, Son of God. Although the FBI believed Yahweh ben Yahweh and his followers were responsible for many murders, they could find no direct evidence linking them to the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In rural Texas, 13-year-old is kidnapped on her way to school. Frantic to find her safely, her millionaire father calls local authorities and the FBI. As police officers and agents conduct aerial surveillance, the father drives hundreds of miles through the night to deliver the ransom cash to a drop that the kidnappers keep changing. In a final effort to save the young girl, investigators must engage in a dangerous firefight with five desperate criminals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, south Miami became the home for a sadistic serial killer. Posing as a fashion photographer, Christopher Wilder lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead. As the FBI closed in around him, Wilder took off on a cross-country murderous rampage. The FBI added Wilder to their Most Wanted Fugitives program. The chase was on, and Wilder ultimately lost.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A day that began with a class field trip ended with a parent's worst nightmare. An 11-year-old girl was missing. Her parents were certain she was no runaway. When they failed to find her in their quiet Illinois neighborhood, they called the police. The FBI soon joined the investigation and family and friends worked with detectives and agents in an urgent search for the missing girl. Faced with conflicting witness reports and false leads, agents wouldn't stop until they found the girl, and the truth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. They soon realized that Dhinsa was more than a crooked businessman, he enforced his mob-like operation with brutal violence, kidnapping and killing errant employees and any family members who questioned their deaths. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: California Highway Patrolman George Gwaltney discovered the body of 23-year-old Robin Bishop near her car on a remote desert highway. Although he proclaimed his innocence the FBI lab experts were able to link tool marks found on the frame with tools found in Gwaltney's home. Through the match of hairs and fibers and a unique test for detecting and identifying sperm, Gwaltney was placed at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a small crime free Indiana town was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. After an officer was gunned down at point-blank range, the psychotic killer led investigators across the Midwest. No one knew where the gunman would strike next, even as his tally of murders and abductions grew. The FBI and state police raced against time to rescue America's heartland from this deranged gunman, before he killed again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By April of 1995 in Washington, D.C., two officers had been shot and one had been murdered. Each had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man sneaked up and opened fire. The FBI was brought in to help catch the cop killer. Agents identified the man who was most likely the killer, but he was nowhere to be found. Months of surveillance operations around the country finally yielded a break; they found their suspect in Baltimore, MD.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On the day before Thanksgiving, a mother discovered her 10 year-old son was missing from his school. No one knew how it could have happened. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. The few leads in the case quickly turned cold and the FBI was called in to aid in the investigation. The search by agents and local authorities would cover a thousand miles to expose the kidnapper and find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 26, 1993, an explosion rocked the World Trade Center parking garage. Six people died and more than a thousand were injured in the bomb blast that tore a 5-story crater in the building. During the investigation, the twisted fender of a rental van was extracted from the rubble. The FBI was able to determine from where the van was rented and by whom. Undercover agents then made their arrests within six days of the bombing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Alabama, a pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country. Eluding authorities, they moved from state to state in a wave of abduction and car theft. When a foreign military officer disappeared along their trail, police and FBI agents wondered if the armed fugitives had become killers. Agents believed the pair were growing more violent and raced to find the escapees before more victims crossed their path.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 80's, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation. Regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. Desperate to stop the violence, local authorities turned to the FBI for help. One dealer bowed to no one. He would stop at nothing to keep detectives and agents from building a case against him. When his organization began targeting the government itself, a city went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June of 1992, masked bandits pulled off a daring mid-day bank robbery. It marked the beginning of a four-year crime wave that rocked Seattle. With each new robbery, the bandits perfected their craft, leaving behind no evidence and escaping with hundreds and thousands of dollars. As the robbers became increasingly violent, pressure mounted to end their reign. With few leads, the FBI forced their own luck. The effort put them face to face with an armed and desperate fugitive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Larry Horn nearly got away with the perfect crime: a multiple murder-for-hire plot that was meticulously planned and executed. Horn's former wife, their 8-year-old son, and a live-in nurse were all brutally murdered by an assassin. In the absence of any significant clues, police in Maryland contacted the FBI to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, the decapitation of a young African-American male led the Miami-Dade police department and the FBI along a trail of murder. Investigators quickly discovered that the victim was a dissident member of a religious sect headed by a man calling himself Yahweh ben Yahweh, or God, Son of God. Although the FBI believed Yahweh ben Yahweh and his followers were responsible for many murders, they could find no direct evidence linking them to the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Alabama, a pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country. Eluding authorities, they moved from state to state in a wave of abduction and car theft. When a foreign military officer disappeared along their trail, police and FBI agents wondered if the armed fugitives had become killers. Agents believed the pair were growing more violent and raced to find the escapees before more victims crossed their path.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When police in a small Washington State community began looking into the disappearance of a 22-year-old woman, they had no way of knowing it would evolve into a nationwide hunt for a sexual sadist. Police knew the killer's identity, but he was a master of disguise. The FBI was called on to help. Agents discovered that several unsuspecting women across the northwest had fallen prey to his charms.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By December 1985, John Gotti had assumed his place as the new Don of the Gambino crime family in New York with the assassination of his boss, Paul Castellano. The Teflon Don felt secure in his position, always remaining one step ahead of the law. But he was also the ongoing obsession of Bruce Mouw, leader of an elite crew of FBI agents formed to bring down the Gambino family. With the help and genius of his Special Projects Squad, bugs were planted in Gotti's most private meeting places.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990's drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods. When a 10-year-old boy fell victim in the crossfire, NYPD detectives and FBI agents clamped down on the problem. They formed a task force called C-11, designed to dismantle the gangs. In an environment of violence and fear it would be difficult to identify, arrest and convict those involved in the secretive and deadly world of big money drugs.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Kokomo, Indiana an unthinkable crime terrified residents. A young woman was snatched from her home on a bright spring morning. Drops of blood and a torn window screen were all that was left to tell authorities what happened to the twenty-one year old victim. After several days of searching, a former FBI profiler narrowed the suspect list to one. Agents and detectives followed the trail across state lines hoping to find the victim before her time ran out.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Memphis, Tennessee, a horrible crime terrified local residents. When a wealthy young mother was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The twenty-five year old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband, and several spurned boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list. To find the woman and her abductor, investigators would have to first determine the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Racial tensions in Buffalo in 1980 were at an all-time high. Six black males had been brutally murdered--two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets. Residents screamed for the police to stop this serial killer. With no suspects, local investigators turned to the FBI. Profilers were able to produce a psychological composite of the type of person the killer would most likely be. Their analysis paid off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: California Highway Patrolman George Gwaltney discovered the body of 23-year-old Robin Bishop near her car on a remote desert highway. Although he proclaimed his innocence the FBI lab experts were able to link tool marks found on the frame with tools found in Gwaltney's home. Through the match of hairs and fibers and a unique test for detecting and identifying sperm, Gwaltney was placed at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: At first it seemed like a tragic hunting accident when 18-year-old David Wilkey was shot to death in rural Virginia in 1983. A tenacious insurance investigator suspected foul play, but he had no direct evidence. Two years later a man contacted North Carolina FBI agents with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire: he claimed he had been hired to murder Wilkey's killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a young boy and his mother were kidnapped and murdered by a vicious drug cartel, FBI agents in the field worked tirelessly to pierce the secretive drug underworld and unearth those involved. Their surveillance and investigative work uncovered a violent trail of murder and greed. FBI agents and forensic examiners pieced together the evidence needed to draw out and then crush the cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the body of exotic dancer Rachel Siani was found beneath a New Jersey bridge. Investigators wondered if she had committed suicide. They discovered that Rachel offered to assist a drunk customer named John 'Jack' Denofa to his motel room.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times, indicating a personal relationship between the victim and the attacker.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Judy Southern arrived home from work and was shot by a gunman waiting within. Her husband Allen arrived shortly afterwards, called 911 to report his wife had been shot and drove her to the hospital.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1986, Gary Dale Larson was stabbed to death in his Edmond, Oklahoma home and then the killer sexually assaulted Larson's girlfriend Janet Haynes.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Dorothy Donovan was murdered in her Dover, Delaware home and police are skeptical when her son Charles Holden stated that she was murdered by a hitchhiker he had picked up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the early hours of a morning just before Christmas, a college co-ed was abducted from the parking lot of her apartment. Her body was found later that day; she'd been sexually assaulted and then shot at point blank range.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a college student is reported missing, police believe their investigation will be like countless others. But when they discover blood spatter in her boyfriend's bedroom, they know this case is going to be different.

programme.title: S13:E16 - Desenmascarado
programme.desc: Un violador en serie anda suelto en una ciudad de Texas. Nadie sabía dónde atacaría a continuación, pero la audacia de sus ataques y la escalada de violencia contra sus víctimas hicieron que encontrarlo fuera imperativo.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim has been stabbed more than thirty times, and the crime scene is awash with her blood. Near her head, police discover a distinctive button with strands of thread still attached.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. When her abandoned car is found, the tank is empty and a gas can she kept in her trunk for emergencies is missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a nine-year-old girl goes missing, police and volunteers spend weeks searching for her. A psychic's vision leads to a field where her body is discovered, along with what investigators hope is enough evidence to help them to also find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Digital enhancement of security camera video shows that what appears to be a casual encounter is actually a forced abduction, leading to murder. When investigators suspect the crimes are not random, they fear a serial killer may be on the loose.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim was a self-made man who never minced words. Perhaps he was murdered by a disgruntled client or employee. Perhaps the racy photos in his safe held the clue to solving the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A family vacation turns into a nightmare when the wife is found face-down in the lake.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman in the back of the truck was flailing her arms, screaming. They thought she was doing something dangerous for the fun of it. But when they found a jacket near a pool of blood, they knew what they'd seen wasn't a joy ride.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When court clerk Peter Porco doesn't report to his work in November 2004, a courts officer is ordered to the Porcos' family home in Bethlehem, NY.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This series profiles intriguing crimes where science catches the bad guy. Follow coroners, medical examiners, physicians, law enforcement officials, journalists and legal experts as they put together the pieces of the crime puzzle.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This episode documents a deadly bombing at a church in Oakwood, Illinois, in December 1997.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This series profiles intriguing crimes where science catches the bad guy. Follow coroners, medical examiners, physicians, law enforcement officials, journalists and legal experts as they put together the pieces of the crime puzzle.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This series profiles intriguing crimes where science catches the bad guy. Follow coroners, medical examiners, physicians, law enforcement officials, journalists and legal experts as they put together the pieces of the crime puzzle.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why the young scientist is gravely ill. When tests finally reveal the cause, it's too late to save him. Police hope that lab analysis of his hair, showing when attempts were made on his life and what was used will lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver, and no witnesses. Was this an ambush or a random attack? Had the victim been abducted or was she dead? The answers lay in a unique clue, so tiny it was measured in millionths of a meter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Even though their daughter had run away before, she'd always come back. Her parents were sure this time would be no different, but they were wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In little more than a month, two women who lived in the same apartment complex were brutally murdered. The similarities of the crime scenes led police to conclude they were dealing with a serial killer who harbored an intense hatred of women.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a college co-ed vanished without a trace, her fellow students were concerned about her safety and their own.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim was well liked and successful, which made the brutality of the crime even harder to understand.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: James Kenneth Elmen Jr. abducted Julie Estes, then 21, from the Southside convenience store where she worked in 1985. He raped and murdered her. Her body was found the next day, but the killing went unsolved until 2003.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tracey Frame, 35, was convicted of killing David Nixon in April 2002 after he planned to end their relationship.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A surgeon is stabbed to death, and the most likely suspect is seen in a restaurant at the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple is found murdered in their Graham, Washington home; detectives discover shoe impressions, a partial palm print and a neighbor's criminal past.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, Brian & Beverly Mauck were found dead in their Graham, Washington home. Using a bloody palm print and shoe prints at the scene, investigators focus their attention on Daniel Tavares, who had previously been inking a tattoo on Brian's back.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 12, 1994, Crystal Perry was viciously murdered in her home in Bridgton, Maine. Michael Hutchinson, 32, was found guilty of murder in the 1994 slaying at Perry's home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Rachael Mullenix, 19, was convicted of stabbing her mother, Barbara Mullenix, on Sept. 13, 2006, and then packing her bloodied corpse in a cardboard box before dumping it in Newport Bay on the California coast.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 20, 2001, Carrie Nelson, then 20, was beaten to death while working alone in a park office. The case went unsolved for six years until May 2007 when police found a match with DNA samples gathered from the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police get a clue about a couple who disappeared on Memorial Day weekend during an in-progress robbery. The evidence found solves a brutal crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The decade-old murder of a young Texas housewife is investigated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, Palm Beach waitress Denise O'Neill is abducted and murdered. Her neighbor Luis Caballero arouses suspicions by his odd behavior toward TV crews covering the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, Shamaia Smith disappeared from the strip club she worked at in East Hartford, CT. Police look at various customers of the strip club, but focus on local businessman Kenneth Otto.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend. There are clues at the scene: bloody footprints and DNA from the victim's rape kit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000,¾Kristine Fitzhugh¾failed to show up for her music class and her husband Ken found her dead at the bottom of the stairs of their Palo Alto home. Upon further investigation, luminol reveals evidence of murder in the kitchen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, Illinois emergency response came upon a garage fire and a man was found crushed beneath a truck.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, the Florida mobile home of Effie and Michael Ratley catches fire and Michael heroically rescues his wife and infant son. A month later, his wife is found beaten to death in a bedroom of his parents' home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2005, David Castor suffered a slow, agonizing death over a period of days. His wife¾Stacey¾maintained it was suicide, even though it was done with antifreeze.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1990s, the media dubbed Richard Rogers the 'Last Call Killer' because he targeted men in gay bars who were obviously intoxicated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Avis Banks and her unborn child were brutally murdered. The body was discovered by her fianc_ Keyon Pittman. Police learn Pittman was having affairs with other women and he became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, when teenager Tara Munsey goes missing after a work shift at a Virginia restaurant, it is unknown if she ran away or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1989, the dead bodies of Joan Rogers and her two teenage daughters were found floating in Tampa Bay.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why the young scientist is gravely ill. When tests finally reveal the cause, it's too late to save him. Police hope that lab analysis of his hair, showing when attempts were made on his life and what was used will lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers, and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This series profiles intriguing crimes where science catches the bad guy. Follow coroners, medical examiners, physicians, law enforcement officials, journalists and legal experts as they put together the pieces of the crime puzzle.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 12, 1994, Crystal Perry was viciously murdered in her home in Bridgton, Maine. Michael Hutchinson, 32, was found guilty of murder in the 1994 slaying at Perry's home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a young girl was discovered on isolated farmland near Delano, California. She had no ID, but police found mailbox keys in the pocket of her jeans. With no other clues, they checked the mailboxes of every apartment building in Delano.

programme.title: El caso Monroy
programme.desc: Un empleado público al borde de la jubilación encuentra un nuevo propósito en la vida en el penal de mujeres, donde ayuda a las internas a recobrar su libertad y retomar sus vidas, pero se convierte en otro abusivo más del corrupto sistema judicial.

programme.title: ¿Quién mató al patrón?
programme.desc: Un detective llega a un pueblo aparentemente tranquilo. Pero, en su primera noche, se produce un asesinato durante una fiesta organizada por la familia más rica del pueblo.

programme.title: Mi hermana es un peligro
programme.desc: La hermana de Carlos lo visita para conocer a su cuñada, pero él descubre que ambas viven un tórrido romance y planea vengarse con un asesino.

programme.title: El corrido de Santa Amalia
programme.desc: Un hombre bueno y trabajador, atormentado por los recuerdos de la infancia, lucha por un amor prohibido, aunque termine en tragedia.

programme.title: Vico C: la vida del filósofo
programme.desc: La historia del cantante Vico C, compositor y pionero de la música urbana en español, desde sus inicios hasta su éxito internacional.

programme.title: El caso Monroy
programme.desc: Un empleado público al borde de la jubilación encuentra un nuevo propósito en la vida en el penal de mujeres, donde ayuda a las internas a recobrar su libertad y retomar sus vidas, pero se convierte en otro abusivo más del corrupto sistema judicial.

programme.title: A 3 caídas
programme.desc: El legendario luchador Tinieblas Jr. va en busca de los secuestradores de su hija con la ayuda de sus amigos.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Jacob pasa la noche con Lea pensando que se trataba de Raquel, ya que sus cuñados pusieron droga en su bebida. Raquel se entera de lo que paso y que por eso su padre la encerró. Esaú golpea brutalmente a Mahalat por hacer daño a Elizaf.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Mahinur y Metin reciben una llamada y deben ir al colegio de Melissa. Öykü y Metin se encuentran en el supermercado, la veterinaria lo reconoce y hablan sobre los padres adoptivos de Melissa. Besime miente a Servicios Sociales.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Manuel tiene argumento para creer que Efraín puede ser un sospechoso en caso del abuso de Valentina. Emilio pierde la cabeza y va con arma a matar a Gregorio.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Enamorados, Lucas le propone a Jade fugarse juntos, pero ella piensa en Latiffa, que teme quedarse sin marido. Sidi Alí sospecha que su sobrina le oculta algo y le tiende una trampa.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: La vida de Yoo-ri cambia de la noche a la mañana. Jung-sun descubre la identidad de la amante de Sung-joon y luego le muestra a su esposo lo que podría pasar en caso de divorcio.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.desc: Santiago cae en una trampa y Antonia se pierde cuando baja al sótano de la jaula.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Diego enfrenta a su padre y lo pone a elegir entre él y Cristina. Leonardo se va con su novia. Lucas busca desesperado a Jade en el desierto, pero una tormenta de arena causa un accidente.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: Seong-jun cuenta la historia de su amor prohibido; Mi-na se encuentra en aguas calientes; Jeong-seon canaliza la ira en venganza.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Melissa le pregunta a Besime si Hazim la lastimó, pero ella lo niega. Besime obliga a Melissa a ayudar con las labores de la casa si quiere ir a la escuela, ella se niega y ella la encierra bajo llave. Görkem va a casa de Metin para ver a Melissa.

programme.title: Apuesta Final
programme.desc: Dae-gil, un príncipe obligado a vivir como un plebeyo, arriesga su vida cuando usa sus habilidades de juego para participar en una partida contra el rey Yeongjo, quien apuesta todo el Reino de Joseon.

programme.title: Sol naciente
programme.desc: Mario y Alicia son amigos de la infancia, él es nieto de migrantes italianos y ella es adoptada por Tanaka, un japonés. Cuando Alicia decide estudiar dos años en Japón, Mario se da cuenta de que lo que siente por ella es más que una amistad. Luego del reencuentro él tiene que lidiar con otro obstáculo, César, que trata de enamorar a la joven, pero con oscuras intenciones. Entonces, los Di Angelo y los Tanaka ponen a prueba sus sentimientos a pesar de las traiciones, las estafas y las mentiras.

programme.title: Orgullo y pasión
programme.desc: Elisabeta es una joven rebelde y llena de sueños que lucha contra el único destino para una mujer de buena familia en su época: casarse. Su osadía y su independencia la meterán en más de un problema.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: La historia del pueblo hebreo tras la muerte de Moisés cuando, liderados por Josué, tendrán que enfrentarse a distintos reinos para tomar Canaán, la tierra que Dios les prometió.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories
programme.desc: From ice hockey to lacrosse to tennis, Baltimore coaches help drive the next generation to find family, inspiration and pride through sports.

programme.title: Free Spirit
programme.desc: Kate explores gin and rituals with a master distiller, talks art and cocktails with an Indigenous painter, and gets a lesson in moving mixology on George R.R. Martin’s adventure train in the high-desert city of Santa Fe.

programme.title: New Hampshire Chronicle
programme.desc: They say there is no such thing as bad art, and Sean is putting that theory to the test, visiting two spots that proudly celebrate some truly questionable creations.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Jessy Melo of Jessy’s Clean Meals is back for redemption. Can Chef Nikki Irving, owner of Los Jefes Comida in Taunton, show how she too, can elevate clean and fresh cooking to a higher level or will the blindfold be a game changer?

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Soon-to-be-married David and Justin both work long hours in education. When the school year is over, they go abroad for adventure. Kinga shows them there is plenty to do in their own backyard in Cincinnati.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Who knew you could explore endless outdoor adventure, a vibrant food and wine scene, crystal blue freshwater, world-class golf, and a charming downtown- all in Northern Michigan? Join host Hannah Cumler as she showcases the best of Traverse City!

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: After giving away thousands of tattoos to Chaos & Kindness fans, Justin and Ryan hit the kitchen to make cookies to share across New Hampshire.

programme.title: LIVE: WMUR News 9
programme.desc: Get the top New Hampshire news of the day. With the latest Manchester news, weather, and sports, WMUR is your local news leader.

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: Grit of Greenwood
programme.desc: Greenwood was a thriving Black district in Tulsa when, 100 years ago, it was destroyed in less than 24 hours.

programme.title: New Hampshire Chronicle
programme.desc: New Hampshire author Dan Brown shares what it took to write his new book, 'The Secret of Secrets'

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: On December 10, 2021, a violent tornado outbreak hit western Kentucky. It was the deadliest weather event in the history of the Commonwealth. But as families faced a bleak holiday season, hope emerged in surprising ways.

programme.title: One Pill Can Kill
programme.desc: Told through the voices of grieving parents, this documentary follows the real and lasting impact of the fentanyl crisis. Capturing the aftermath of sudden loss, the search for answers, and the transformation of grief into public advocacy.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Social butterfly, Metzli is ready for the next phase of her life when it comes to love. Will she grow with Matt, shake things up with Greg or harness a connection with David?

programme.title: Unbelievable

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Restaurants are back in a big way – and we’re touring the newest spots on the scene. Global meets local in Dorchester, where two couples have opened “cafe by day, restaurant by night,” Comfort Kitchen. Pubs may be a dime a dozen, but the new Irish-inspired restaurant in downtown Boston, The Dubliner, is winning raves for the elevated menu. We sample Italian in Woburn, Japanese in the Fenway, and find smoothies in (of all places) the North End. Finally, for dessert – French delights at SALT Patisserie in Newton.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There’s far more to education these days than reading, writing, and arithmetic. Tonight we meet students and educators who are exploring new ground. From homeschooling alternatives that grew out of the pandemic, to the rising demand for vocational education, to a program wooing older students back to college (for free!) local students are finding new and creative pathways to success.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s the 100th anniversary of Black History Month – but it’s the nation’s 250th. Tonight we share some lesser-known stories that underscore the role of Black Americans in revolutionary times.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Game Gone Wild: The Socker Slam Story
programme.desc: A stranger than fiction documentary that chronicles a semi-scripted extreme sporting league that premiered in 2000. Socker Slam took elements of wrestling, rugby and “butts up” shenanigans and body slammed them into a soccer drama.

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
programme.desc: (2022) The disappearance of Johnny Gosch is one of Iowa's longest running, and most notorious mysteries. In 1982, the 12-year-old paperboy in West Des Moines vanished without a trace. Forty years later, KCCI uncovers where the investigation stands.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When you’ve won an Emmy, two Grammys, a Tony, and an Oscar, what could possibly be next? For actress/singer/producer Jennifer Hudson it’s a new daytime talk show, and Shayna Seymour sits down with the superstar here in Boston for a chat about this new phase of her career. Tonight we also catch up with New Englanders who, like JHud, are pursuing new passions, including a top college football player who discovered his love of painting after suffering an injury; an aspiring doctor who left the medical field to become a baker; and two brothers who started a pizza business on wheels.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s springtime in Niantic, where Main Street hugs the coastline, book lovers find refuge at the state’s largest used bookstore, and locals find respite at Hole-in-the-Wall beach. Tonight Nicole Estaphan explores this magical village in the town of East Lyme, strolls its beloved boardwalk, and connects with groups working to protect local wildlife. She also visits a local prison introducing inmates to rehabilitation through gardening, and discovers where Michael Douglas earned some cash before his acting career.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A breakthrough medical procedure, feeding penguins at the New England Aquarium, serving up doggie ice cream, and connecting with an inspiring woman who is overcoming the odds. Tonight, Nicole Estaphan shares her favorite stories of 2021.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you’ve had more than your share of sweet goodies this holiday season tonight Nicole Estaphan treats us to a helping of eye candy – reintroducing us to an artist working on the largest indoor collaborative mural in the world, and a sign painter bringing beauty to Main Street. Nicole recalls the time she spent with a refugee and his family who have resettled in Massachusetts after fleeing Afghanistan amid the fall of Kabul. We hear the inspiring story of a young woman who has battled opioid addiction and is now thriving in the Ivy League. And yes, we get another serving of coffee from Nicole’s show on java.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole's Favorite's 2023

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Been a while since you’ve had a night out? Tonight we check out live music, cooking classes, and the cocktail scene in Boston. For a taste of the city we hit up Pescador in Kenmore Square and Hue, a new supper club in the Back Bay, as well as locally-owned Roundhead Brewing in Hyde Park. Erika Tarantal takes aim at Flight Club, a darts lounge in the Seaport. Shayna Seymour drops in on an evening cooking class at Shiso Kitchen in Somerville, while Bonde in Cambridge offers after-hours wine tastings. And for a little live music we get rocking at Fallout Shelter in Norwood.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from WCVB NewsCenter 5.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: In “Paradise City” Anthony Everett finds a hub of culture, arts, education, and community. He walks historic Main Street with the mayor, and pops into the eatery locals fondly refer to as Northampton’s “living room” – Joe’s Cafe. We check in with Historic Northampton, working to preserve the city’s roots (including a 200-year-old barn) and visit with local artists and musicians who make up this diverse city. Need a break from winter? We step into Smith College’s Botanic Greenhouse. Finally, we meet volunteers building community by feeding those in need.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Panoramic views, quaint villages, and creative artists and craftspeople -- the Northeast Kingdom is quintessential Vermont and Nicole Estaphan is hopping on a mountain bike to take in the sights. She meets a California couple now living out their dream as New England innkeepers, makes a pilgrimage to a church-turned-brewery, and samples “the best espresso on the planet.” Nicole also makes a stop at a little piece of doggie heaven.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From the capital city of Halifax, to the coastal village of Peggy’s Cove, we travel back to this Canadian maritime province that is so closely tied to New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: At Chronicle you know we love food – almost as much as we love sharing stories about food with you. Tonight we serve up more delicious bites from the Chronicle pantry to whet your appetite for our prime-time special, at 8 p.m. immediately following tonight’s show. Yes, friends, it’s time to EAT!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The pull of the ocean is powerful here in New England, and Erika Tarantal is checking in on the health of the salt water that helps sustain us. Tonight Erika joins a Sandwich lobsterman who helps oceanographers measure water temperature, wades the Wellfleet clam flats, joins a beach brigade in Hyannis to clean up plastic and debris, and walks the North Shore’s Great Marsh with volunteers working to restore the natural ecosystem. She talks to experts, innovators, and volunteers who are taking the ocean’s temperature - and doing something about it.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Lego Masters, Treasure Hunters, Dog Treat Deliveries; Chronicle explores, ‘Odd Jobs.’

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A mermaid, a ventriloquist, and an organ grinder are among the unusual jobs we’re checking out tonight. Nicole Estaphan meets a professional mermaid who teaches others to swim like the sirens of the sea, and an organ grinder (no monkey) who is keeping a musical tradition alive. On a grimier note, we meet Somerville’s “rat czar” and a couple who make a living cleaning up after your pooch.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan visits New England museums that house PEZ dispensers, lost toys – pretty much anything but oil paintings.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett spends a day on the water with shark experts spotting and tagging sharks. He also tries out a new style of personal flotation device; heads out on a New Hampshire lake to monitor water quality; and meets the New Bedford woman helping to change the face of the blue economy as head of her family’s seafood business.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Massachusetts is home to the largest compass manufacturer in the U.S. Tonight Anthony Everett visits this Pembroke company that has been guiding mariners for generations. Also tonight, Anthony catches up with students experiencing the outdoors on one of the Boston Harbor Islands, which has a new name but the same mission. He meets scientists from the University of New Hampshire who are studying why the Greenland ice cap is melting so quickly, and others who are studying the New England snow pack and what it can tell us about climate change.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s Tiki time in Salem! Hit the high seas on a unique Tiki Boat.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads out in search of more coastal inspiration. He meets a North Shore craftsman who repairs and builds classic, canvas-wrapped canoes in his Groveland workshop – and visits a Newbury shop that sells the modern variety.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From John Williams to Ruth Pointer to Peter Wolf, the Boston area has been home to incredible music-makers of all genres. Tonight, a look at Chronicle sit-downs with some local musical greats!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The Parker River in Newbury Anthony visits the “intergalactic headquarters of the hoop-making business.” In Plymouth, Anthony joins researchers who are working to conserve vulnerable bird species – including American oystercatchers & piping plovers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett is back with more stories from the shore – lake, ocean, and otherwise.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Before summer ends Anthony Everett is bringing us more stories from New England waters. He’s cruising Boston Harbor on a boat booked through an app that makes access to the water easier for non-boaters. Anthony joins UNH scientists studying invasive seaweed species and their impact on marine life. Speaking of invasive species, he samples a new whiskey made from green crabs! Finally, on Lake Champlain in Vermont Anthony discovers a unique kayak-building program for kids.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Juvenile Lifers: Road to Redemption
programme.desc: (2019) Juvenile Lifers: Road to Redemption focuses on people who were sentenced to life in prison as juveniles. WGAL News 8's Matt Barcaro examines how these these former offenders are re-entering society and the reaction from victims' families.

programme.title: The Long Road Home
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, the communities of Western North Carolina were forever changed. "The Long Road Home" is a WXII 12 original documentary that captures powerful stories of loss, hope, and recovery.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s not hard to find wooden boat builders in New England. Finding one who chopped down the trees himself - and who has never actually sailed - is another thing altogether. That’s just one of the stories Anthony Everett is bringing us in a July edition of On the Water. He visits The Sailing Museum, now open in Newport, R.I., and hits up a new dining destination in a historic seaside home, the Winsor House in Duxbury. Anthony also meets author Eric Jay Dolin who shares fascinating facts from his new book about Revolutionary-era “privateers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: On the Charles River, Anthony Everett meets a champion rower from Ukraine who has found refuge from his wartorn country here in Boston and is now sharing his rowing gifts with students at Community Rowing, Inc. In Gloucester, Anthony visits the biggest exhibition ever mounted by the Cape Ann Museum, which sheds light on artist Edward Hopper’s transformative years spent on the North Shore. Then, is it a boat? A plane? No, it’s a “seaglider” – a new category of vehicle that may soon revolutionize regional travel. Anthony explains, as he brings us these and more seaworthy stories tonight.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett is back with stories inspired by the sea. In Boston’s North End, he visits the historic Mariner’s House, which has been meeting the needs of seafarers since 1847. Are electric outboard motors the wave of the future for boaters? Anthony meets the team at Bristol, R.I.’s Flux Marine, which is developing the technology. In Gloucester, Anthony meets locals working to keep alive the golden age of the fishing schooner. Finally, Anthony introduces us to “BEN” – a small vessel with the power to help map the vast ocean floor.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Explore the RV lifestyle and the rise in popularity of road trips.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Erika Tarantal catches up with people who are trying to level the playing field. Erika discovers a gear “library” offered by the Appalachian Mountain Club, & a local nonprofit that helps those with physical disabilities bike, hike, rock-climb, & ski.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Misty mornings and striking views – we find both on the Outer Cape in the fall. Tonight we venture past the Cape’s crooked elbow and meet a local woman who is determined to trek every hiking trail on Cape Cod, and offers tips for heading “off the beaten path.” We fuel up with food that is decidedly not lobster rolls or fried clams (try, global cuisine at the newest restaurant in Orleans). We check out a historic inn in Eastham, and take in beautiful views from a new state-of-the-art inclined elevator connecting the Pilgrim Monument to downtown Provincetown.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from WCVB NewsCenter 5.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Kindergarten in the woods? Talk about thinking “outside the box.” Tonight, Nicole Estaphan meets people who are teaching, creating, and healing with methods that are a bit unorthodox. Nicole meets those kindergarteners who spend each school day outdoors (as long as it’s above 10 degrees!). She meets an artist whose work is based on mathematical equations. And she goes inside the box with a local entrepreneur who plans New England road trips in a box.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Keep an eye out because your next walk in the woods may just turn into a stroll through an art installation! In Kensington, N.H., you can touch, explore, and even walk on world-class art pieces at Alnoba. Creativity knows no bounds at Lincoln’s deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum, and at Frog Pond Farm in Harvard, visitors can dance to the beat of their own drum. Meanwhile, Ted Reinstein discovers that to enjoy an outdoor art exhibit at the Gardens at Elm Banks, strangely, he needs to be... on his phone.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Fresh from their beds - or fried to a crisp - New Englanders love oysters, and the local bivalve business is booming. Tonight Nicole Estaphan digs into the history of oysters, meets local farmers, and learns the art of shucking. She meets members of the Mashpee Wampanoag tribe who are continuing a cherished tradition of harvesting shellfish and working to develop the First Light Shellfish Farm. Nicole stops by EBO & Co. in East Boston, an eclectic purveyor of oysters (and much more), as well as family-owned Naked Oyster in Hyannis - a restaurant where the oysters hail from the owner’s own farm.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The New York Times named Panama #4 on its list of places to go in 2019. Tonight, NewsCenter 5’s Mike Beaudet gives us a tour of this fascinating country, from the bustling, cosmopolitan capital of Panama City with its towering skyscrapers – to a hike in the rainforest where locals are working to protect the ecosystem. And no visit to Panama would be complete without a visit to the historic Panama Canal. Join Mike and a group of Northeastern University journalism students who spent a month studying the culture and bringing back stories of Panama and its people.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The New York Times named Panama #4 on its list of places to go in 2019. Tonight 5 Investigates’ Mike Beaudet gives us a tour of this fascinating country, from the bustling, cosmopolitan capital of Panama City – to a hike in the rainforest where locals are working to protect the ecosystem. And no visit to Panama would be complete without a visit to the historic Panama Canal. Join Mike and a group of Northeastern University journalism students who spent a month studying the culture and bringing back stories of Panama and its people.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It is one of the most difficult subjects to discuss within families, therefore the one too often avoided. Tonight we are talking about death – and the steps people can take to ease the transition. We meet a doctor who is working to coach other medical providers on how to discuss death with patients – while dealing with his own terminal diagnosis.

programme.desc: The Golden State Killer, also known as the East Area Rapist, remained a mystery for years.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Maverick Meyers is a shy 17 year old gamer who spends 8 hours a day on his self-built computer. Kinga meets Maverick in Clermont, Florida to take him out of his comfort zone on an outing he’ll never forget.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Chef David Carmine and Chef Kay Stewart have to channel their inner Yinzer to whip up some Pittsburgh-themed dishes that pay homage to the city's eclectic cuisine.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Hang with a viral sensation in his taxidermy workshop. Watch a bakery turn into a Burmese restaurant. Load up a picnic basket with chicken and ‘dippity dips.’ Drink all the wines, and then sip on what a local calls a ‘sober-up beer.’

programme.title: Local Love
